lec15-tree-thinking: Darwin sketched the first phylogenetic tree in his notebook in 1837 — evolution is BRANCHING, not a ladder. COMPONENTS: TIPS (terminal species/genes), BRANCHES (lineages through time), NODES (split points = speciation events), ROOT (ancestor, determined by outgroup), CLADE (ancestor + all descendants = monophyletic group), SISTER TAXA (lineages sharing immediate common ancestor, one node apart). ROBBINS SNAIL ISLAND EXAMPLE: Population on island 1 colonizes island 2 (pink evolves), then island 3 (high-spire), then island 4. Current island 1 snails are NOT ancestors of islands 3 and 4 — evolutionary forces did not stop after divergence. Populations on islands 3 and 4 are more closely related to each other than either is to population 1. Trees can be drawn many ways without changing phylogeny — rotations at any node are free; only the TOPOLOGY matters. NEVER read trees as progress: every living species is equally evolved.
lec15-cladistics-synapomorphy: SYSTEMATICS: the scientific field of classification. SYNAPOMORPHY = shared DERIVED character, the key tool for building clades. SYMPLESIOMORPHY (ancestral character shared broadly) is UNINFORMATIVE for relationships within a group. CLADISTICS uses synapomorphies to construct phylogenetic trees. MONOPHYLETIC group = ancestor + ALL descendants (valid clade). PARAPHYLETIC = ancestor + SOME descendants (invalid: reptiles excluding birds). POLYPHYLETIC = multiple unrelated ancestors (warm-blooded animals = birds + mammals = two independent origins). Birds ARE theropod dinosaurs. To make reptiles monophyletic we must include birds. Aves is a lineage within Sauropsida. Insect character matrix: 5 taxa give 105 possible trees; 9 taxa give 2 million. Computer algorithms are required for large trees.
lec15-homoplasy-convergence: PRINCIPLE OF PARSIMONY: the preferred phylogenetic hypothesis minimizes total evolutionary change (Occam razor applied to trees). HOMOPLASY = character-state similarity NOT due to shared descent. Two types: (1) EVOLUTIONARY REVERSALS (derived character reverts to ancestral state); (2) CONVERGENT EVOLUTION (independent origins of similar traits from similar environmental pressures). Camera eye in vertebrates vs cephalopods = CONVERGENT (different wiring, vertebrates have blind spot, octopus does not). Whale streamlined body shape is CONVERGENT with sharks despite whales being mammals. Whale closest LIVING relative is the HIPPOPOTAMUS (confirmed by molecular + fossil data). Limblessness in squamates evolved INDEPENDENTLY in snakes, glass lizards, legless skinks, and amphisbaenians. Boas and pythons retain vestigial hind-limb spurs. Molecular data has overturned many morphology-based groupings.
lec15-fins-to-limbs: COELACANTHS were thought extinct since the Cretaceous, then discovered ALIVE in 1938 off South Africa. They are one of the closest living cousins of tetrapods; their fin bones are HOMOLOGOUS with tetrapod forelimb bones. LUNGFISH (Australian, South American, African) are less closely related. PHYLOGENETIC PREDICTION: a transitional fossil should exist in mid-Devonian coastal wetland rocks between known fish and early tetrapods (Acanthostega, Ichthyostega). NEIL SHUBIN and TED DAESCHLER searched mid-Devonian rocks on Ellesmere Island (Northern Canada). TIKTAALIK (2004): weight-bearing elbows, bending wrist (could do push-ups), NECK (lost bony operculum so head moves independently of body), eyes on top of head, lateral line still present, still has fins + webbing. Series most to least fish-like: Eusthenopteron, Panderichthys, Tiktaalik, Acanthostega, Ichthyostega, terrestrial tetrapods. Tiktaalik is NOT our direct ancestor — it is a COUSIN preserving transitional anatomy.
lec15-feathers-exaptation: ARCHAEOPTERYX (discovered 1860, ~145 Mya) is the classic transitional form: feathers AND teeth, claws on wings, long bony tail. But feathers are found in NON-FLYING theropod dinosaurs whose arms were too short for flight — so feathers evolved for OTHER functions first. PRE-FLIGHT FUNCTIONS: species recognition, mate attraction, INSULATION, egg incubation. Oviraptor (discovered in brooding position, 1993) incubated eggs with its feathered arms. VELOCIRAPTOR QUILL NODES: the ulna of Velociraptor shows regularly-spaced bumps identical to the quill nodes in modern living birds — direct fossil evidence of feathers in a non-flying dinosaur. EXAPTATION = a trait that originated performing one function and was later CO-OPTED for a new function. Feathers: insulation/display then FLIGHT. WAIR hypothesis: flapping helped juveniles run up steep surfaces, selecting for stronger flight stroke. Another exaptation: SWIM BLADDERS — early fish had primitive lungs for gulping air in low-oxygen water; many fish modified them into swim bladders for buoyancy. Swim bladders are HOMOLOGOUS with tetrapod lungs.
ch13-species-concepts: BIOLOGICAL SPECIES CONCEPT (Mayr 1942): groups of actually or potentially interbreeding natural populations reproductively isolated from other such groups. FAILS for asexuals (bdelloid rotifers), fossils (trilobites), allopatric populations that never meet, and hybridizers. Elk and red deer interbreed in captivity but not in the wild — BSC ambiguous. PHYLOGENETIC SPECIES CONCEPT: the smallest monophyletic group with shared derived characters. Problem: species counts depend on WHICH characters are chosen (Robbins called this garbage in garbage out). GENERAL LINEAGE CONCEPT (de Queiroz): species are separately evolving metapopulation lineages. Integrates multiple criteria and flags borderline cases. The mule exemplifies BSC at the edges: horse x donkey produces a viable but sterile hybrid, so the parents ARE separate species. Anopheles gambiae complex: morphologically identical mosquitoes that do not interbreed.
ch13-isolating-barriers: Reproductive isolation is a TIMELINE: PRE-MATING, POST-MATING PREZYGOTIC, POSTZYGOTIC. PRE-MATING: (1) HABITAT isolation (Rhagoletis apple vs hawthorn races); (2) TEMPORAL isolation (coral mass-spawning at different times); (3) BEHAVIORAL isolation (firefly flash patterns); (4) MECHANICAL isolation (duck genitalia; damselfly appendages). POST-MATING PREZYGOTIC: (5) GAMETIC isolation (sea urchin BINDIN matches only species-specific VERL egg receptor). POSTZYGOTIC: (6) HYBRID INVIABILITY (Mimulus Hms1/Hms2 BDM incompatibility kills hybrid seedlings); (7) HYBRID STERILITY (mule: horse 64 chromosomes x donkey 62 gives mule 63 so meiosis fails); (8) HYBRID BREAKDOWN (F1 fine, F2 collapses). COYNE and ORR: PREZYGOTIC isolation evolves FASTER than postzygotic in sympatry due to REINFORCEMENT.
ch13-speciation-modes: Four models: (1) ALLOPATRIC — geographic barrier splits population (MOST COMMON mode). (2) PARAPATRIC — adjacent populations with reduced gene flow along an environmental gradient. (3) SYMPATRIC — divergence without geographic barrier; requires strong disruptive selection. Controversial but documented: Rhagoletis pomonella apple/hawthorn host races in sympatry. (4) ECOLOGICAL SPECIATION — reproductive isolation evolves as a byproduct of divergent selection on different niches. ALLOPOLYPLOIDY produces INSTANT speciation via chromosome doubling — best documented in plants (Tragopogon; bread wheat hexaploid) but also insects, frogs, fish, some mammals. REINFORCEMENT: selection favors stronger PREZYGOTIC barriers in sympatry when hybrids are unfit. ISOLATION BY DISTANCE: populations spread across a gradient diverge gradually.
ch13-bdm-cryptic: BATESON-DOBZHANSKY-MULLER (BDM) INCOMPATIBILITIES: ancestor has alleles A1B1 (both fine). Population 1 evolves A2 (fine with B1). Population 2 evolves B2 (fine with A1). Hybrids inherit A2+B2 — a combination NEVER TESTED BY SELECTION — which turns out incompatible. Each allele is fine in its own lineage but deadly in combination. BDM incompatibilities SNOWBALL: more generations in isolation = more diverged loci = more potential incompatible pairs. EXAMPLES: Mimulus Hms1/Hms2; Drosophila Hmr/Lhr. CRYPTIC SPECIES are morphologically identical but reproductively isolated. Anopheles gambiae complex. DNA barcoding (Hebert and Janzen 2004 Astraptes butterflies = ~10 cryptic species). Apanteles leucostigmus wasp: 1 morphological species is 32 cryptic species by barcoding. For bacteria, 16S 97% threshold is problematic (S. pneumoniae vs S. mitis are 99% identical at 16S).
ch13-empirical-cases: PANAMA SHRIMP (Knowlton): Isthmus of Panama closed ~3 Mya, splitting Atlantic and Pacific snapping shrimp. Molecular data showed sister pairs diverged at closure. When placed in lab aquaria together, sister pairs REFUSED to interbreed — confirmed reproductive isolation. HAWAIIAN LAUPALA CRICKETS (Shaw): 37 species on the Big Island, diverging at ~10x the average arthropod rate. Males produce species-specific pulse songs by RUBBING LEGS; females respond. Robbins: similar to frogs — probably the same general mechanism. TWO INDEPENDENT Big Island colonizations visible in phylogeny. QTL mapping: male song AND female preference map to the SAME locus (single mutation shifts both). POLAR BEARS (Lorenzen 2014, Liu 2014): diverged from brown bears ~479-343 kya; strong positive selection on APOB gene (cholesterol binding — fat seal blubber diet). ONE-WAY gene flow from brown into polar bear, yet polar bears remain distinct. MIMULUS POLLINATOR ISOLATION (Schemske and Bradshaw 1999): M. cardinalis (red, hummingbird) vs M. lewisii (pink, bumblebee). Single QTL controls flower color. ASTRAPTES BUTTERFLIES: DNA barcoding revealed ~10 cryptic species.
biogeography-extinction: BIOGEOGRAPHY studies species distributions and processes shaping them. ISLAND BIOGEOGRAPHY (MacArthur and Wilson 1967): species number depends on island area and distance from source. Oceanic islands have extreme ENDEMISM via founder effects + adaptive radiation. ADAPTIVE RADIATION: rapid diversification into many niches. Examples: Darwin finches, cichlid fish (African Rift Lakes 1000+ species), Hawaiian honeycreepers, placental mammals after K-Pg. CONTINENTAL DRIFT explains DISJUNCT DISTRIBUTIONS. BIG FIVE MASS EXTINCTIONS: end-Ordovician (~85% marine), late Devonian, end-Permian (~96%, Siberian Traps, Great Dying), end-Triassic, K-T (66 Mya Chicxulub). Current sixth extinction estimates 100-1000x background rate. WALLACE LINE through Lombok Strait: sharp Asian/Australian faunal break despite islands only ~35 km apart, because deep trench remained flooded during Pleistocene glacials. GREAT AMERICAN BIOTIC INTERCHANGE ~3 Mya: N American fauna outcompeted S American because N America had been connected to Eurasia. PUNCTUATED EQUILIBRIUM (Gould and Eldredge 1972): long stasis punctuated by rapid speciation.
conservation: Conservation biology applies evolution to preserve biodiversity. SMALL-POPULATION GENETICS: INBREEDING DEPRESSION (increased homozygosity exposes deleterious recessives); LOSS OF ADAPTIVE POTENTIAL; GENETIC DRIFT fixing mildly deleterious alleles. HUMANS AS SELECTIVE FORCE: Trophy hunting on Ram Mountain bighorn sheep selected against large-horned males — average horn size declined ~20% in 30 years. Antibiotic overuse selects for MRSA. Fishing removes large individuals then shifts life history to earlier maturity. CHEETAH BOTTLENECK (~10,000 years ago): unrelated cheetahs accept skin grafts — massive disease vulnerability. FLORIDA PANTHER GENETIC RESCUE (1995): 8 Texas cougars reversed heart defects and reproductive failure. 50/500 RULE: effective pop size >=50 prevents short-term inbreeding; >=500 maintains long-term evolutionary potential. OUTBREEDING DEPRESSION: mixing distant populations disrupts locally adapted gene combinations.
human-evolution: Hominin evolution spans ~7 million years. ARDIPITHECUS (~4.4 Mya): partial biped. AUSTRALOPITHECUS (3-4 Mya): LUCY (Au. afarensis, 3.2 Mya, Ethiopia) — bipedal pelvis, ~430 cc brain (chimp-sized). CONFIRMS BIPEDALISM BEFORE BRAIN EXPANSION. Laetoli footprints (3.6 Mya). HOMO HABILIS (~2.4 Mya): first stone tools. HOMO ERECTUS (~1.9 Mya): first major out-of-Africa dispersal, controlled fire. HOMO SAPIENS (~300 kya origin, ~195 kya modern form). NEANDERTHALS (~400-40 kya Eurasia): non-African modern humans carry ~1-4% Neanderthal DNA from interbreeding. DENISOVANS: Melanesian and Papuan populations carry ~3-6% Denisovan DNA. HOMO FLORESIENSIS (~50 kya Flores): ~1 m tall, insular dwarfism. MITOCHONDRIAL EVE: ~200 kya in Africa. OBSTETRICAL DILEMMA: human babies skull near maximum the pelvis can accommodate — trade-off. LACTASE PERSISTENCE evolved INDEPENDENTLY in multiple cattle-herding populations via different mutations.
evolutionary-medicine: Evolutionary medicine applies evolution to health. Four frameworks: (1) TRADE-OFFS — selection optimizes early reproduction at cost of late-life health (antagonistic pleiotropy). (2) MISMATCH — ancestral metabolism and behavior mismatched to modern industrial environments; explains obesity, T2D, allergies, myopia. (3) HOST-PATHOGEN ARMS RACES — pathogens evolve rapidly (MRSA, HIV, flu). (4) DEFENSES MISTAKEN FOR PATHOLOGY — fever, morning sickness, inflammation are evolved responses. SICKLE CELL BALANCED POLYMORPHISM: HbS homozygotes suffer anemia but heterozygotes have ~25% protection against severe malaria; in malaria-endemic regions HbS is maintained at ~10-20% frequency. MORNING SICKNESS (Profet): evolved to make pregnant women avoid toxins during first trimester. MRSA evolves rapidly in hospitals due to strong selection plus many susceptible hosts plus short generations plus large populations. CFTR cystic fibrosis mutation may have had heterozygote advantage historically (cholera or typhoid resistance). FEVER is a defense not a disease.
